title: Lane-write ergonomics: resolveMutationTarget auto-resolves calling session to its bound lane when --lane omitted, symmetric with read-path resolvePipeline
action: Per D7: reads auto-resolve session -> bound lane -> default (resolvePipeline, state.mjs:1255-1291) but writes (resolveMutationTarget, bee.mjs:1903-1920) require explicit --lane; a lane-bound session that forgets the flag silently writes the default record. PLAN-CHECK W3: resolveMutationTarget has FOUR callers, all get the new resolution: state set (:1956), state gate (:2184), state scribing-run (:2436), state advisor-ref record (:2635) — the fourth was previously unnamed; include it deliberately and cover it. Make the write path symmetric: when --lane is omitted, resolve the calling session identity (self-resolved at operation moment, B22 — same mechanism the read path uses) and, if that session is bound to a lane, target that lane record; explicit --lane always wins; an unbound session targets the default record exactly as today; missing/corrupt lane still refuses loudly with zero writes (B12/B13). The --owner contract is unchanged and is checked against the SELECTED record's pre-mutation phase (fsh-4 — keep it). Add an escape hatch --no-lane to force the default record from a bound session, documented in the verb schema. Update the four verbs' descriptions in the command registry to state the new resolution order. Extend test_cli_state.mjs: bound session + omitted --lane writes the LANE record; unbound session unchanged; explicit --lane wins; --no-lane forces default; corrupt lane refuses; advisor-ref record follows the same resolution (one row). Regen obligation inside THIS cell: node scripts/render_plugin_skill_trees.mjs && node skills/bee-hive/scripts/onboard_bee.mjs --repo-root . --apply && node scripts/release_manifest.mjs --write.

truths: write-path resolution order matches read-path: explicit flag > session-bound lane > default
unbound sessions see zero behavior change
lane never borrows default authority; refuse-loud rules intact
artifacts: test_cli_state.mjs rows for all five scenarios
prohibitions: never auto-adopt a lane for a session that is not bound to it
never silently fall back to default on a corrupt lane record

trace.outcome: Write-path lane resolution is now symmetric with the read path (D7): resolveMutationTarget resolves explicit --lane > calling session's bound lane (identity self-resolved at operation moment via resolveSessionId, B22) > default record, across all FOUR callers (state set/gate/scribing-run/advisor-ref record, plan-check W3). --no-lane escape hatch forces the default record from a bound session; --lane+--no-lane is refused. A bound session's missing/corrupt lane refuses loudly with zero writes (B12/B13, never silent fallback). --owner stays checked against the SELECTED record's pre-mutation phase (fsh-4), and set --feature is refused on an auto-resolved lane target too (identity-rename guard extended). Unbound sessions byte-unchanged. Registry descriptions of the four verbs state the resolution order + --no-lane. Regen chain run (render_plugin_skill_trees, onboard --apply, release_manifest --write).
trace.verification_evidence:
{
  "tests_inspected": "test_cli_state.mjs pre-existing lane rows (fsh-4 owner-from-lane + byte-isolation, si-1 lane-close debt rows) and test_bee_cli.mjs registry-example rows for state.set/gate/scribing-run --lane",
  "tests_added_or_changed": "5 new D7 rows in skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_cli_state.mjs: (1) bound session + omitted --lane writes the LANE record across set/gate/scribing-run, --owner judged against the lane pre-mutation phase, --feature refused naming --no-lane; (2) unbound session (and identity with no session record) unchanged — default record, no lane note; (3) explicit --lane wins over the binding, --no-lane forces default, --no-lane+--lane refused; (4) corrupt bound lane refuses loudly (zero writes, corrupt bytes untouched) and missing bound lane refuses naming ghost-lane + --no-lane; (5) advisor-ref record follows the same resolution onto the lane with lane-bound anchors",
  "red_failure_evidence": "git stash of skills/bee-hive/templates/bee.mjs (pre-change code) with the new tests in place: 74 passed, 4 failed — rows 1/3/4/5 failed exactly (bound-session writes landed on the default record, --no-lane unknown-flag, corrupt lane fell through); row 2 (unbound unchanged) passed on old code as designed, proving zero behavior change for unbound sessions",
  "verification_run": "node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_cli_state.mjs && node scripts/release_manifest.mjs --check -> 78 passed, 0 failed; release_manifest --check: 510 file(s) match stored manifest. Adjacent registry-scoped suite node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_bee_cli.mjs -> 287 passed, 0 failed",
  "consults": "none — no Advisor line in the dispatch"
}

PASS  bee.mjs state with no verb prints a Use: line listing all five verbs and exits non-zero
PASS  P0 (codex-loop-p0): start-feature is the atomic idle->exploring entry; the naive owner=exploring set from idle is refused
PASS  codex-loop (advisor #54): start-feature hands off FORWARD — its next_action never orders another hive route
PASS  bee.mjs state set writes only the provided fields and creates state.json on a fresh repo
PASS  bee.mjs state set rejects an unknown phase (isKnownPhase, not the bare PHASES array) and leaves the file untouched
PASS  bee.mjs state set refuses to mutate a present-but-corrupt state.json (review P1-1: never clobber to defaults)
PASS  bee.mjs state set accepts the compounding-complete terminal alias (isKnownPhase, not PHASES) — from compounding, per the tail guard
PASS  bee.mjs state set preserves unrelated fields (workers, cells, last_scribing_run) byte-for-byte
PASS  bee.mjs state set requires the selected record pre-phase owner and refuses missing/mismatched ownership with zero writes
PASS  bee.mjs state set rolls ownership with phase, never persists owner, and accepts legacy valid records
PASS  bee.mjs state set derives ownership from the selected lane and isolates lane/default bytes
PASS  D7: a lane-bound session with --lane omitted writes the LANE record across set/gate/scribing-run, with --owner judged against the lane pre-mutation phase and --feature refused
PASS  D7: an unbound session (or an identity with no session record) with --lane omitted writes the default record exactly as before
PASS  D7: explicit --lane always wins over the session binding, and --no-lane forces the default record from a bound session
PASS  D7: a bound session whose lane record is corrupt or missing refuses loudly with zero writes — never a silent fall-back to the default record
PASS  D7: advisor-ref record follows the same write-path resolution — a bound session with --lane omitted records onto the LANE record
PASS  bee.mjs state set treats a missing/invalid stored phase as corrupt before ownership and leaves bytes untouched
PASS  bee.mjs state gate approves a named gate and is idempotent (same call twice = identical file)
PASS  bee.mjs state gate rejects an unknown gate name and a non-boolean --approved
PASS  shipped routing callers declare their pre-phase owner and independent review has no generic state-set caller
PASS  bee.mjs state worker add -> update -> remove -> clear round-trips and preserves unrelated fields
PASS  bee.mjs state worker add rejects an unknown tier
PASS  bee.mjs state worker prune deletes only capped/orphan transients and keeps open-cell, active-worker (dotted ids included), subdir, and non-transient files
PASS  bee.mjs state worker prune --dry-run reports the exact same candidate set and deletes nothing
PASS  bee.mjs state worker prune rejects unknown flags (a --dryrun typo must never delete) and non-prune verbs reject --dry-run
PASS  bee.mjs state worker prune fails closed when state.workers is not an array (semantic corruption, valid JSON)
PASS  bee.mjs state worker prune over a corrupt state.json exits non-zero and deletes nothing (readStateStrict before any rm)
PASS  bee.mjs state worker prune with no .bee/workers dir succeeds with 0 pruned, and the unknown-action Use: line lists prune
PASS  bee.mjs state scribing-run stamps the exact key set from bee-scribing SKILL.md:112 including an ISO-precise at
PASS  bee.mjs state scribing-run accepts a single descriptive area with no comma (real-world shape)
PASS  bee.mjs state rejects an unknown verb with a Use: line, exit non-zero
PASS  start-feature (lib): succeeds from idle with no leftover work, resets all four gates and writes feature/mode/phase in one call
PASS  start-feature (lib): a prior feature carrying approved gates never lets the new feature inherit them
PASS  bee.mjs state start-feature requires --feature
PASS  bee.mjs state start-feature rejects a phase outside the closed vocabulary, zero mutations
PASS  bee.mjs state start-feature refuses when the current phase is not idle/terminal, zero mutations
PASS  bee.mjs state start-feature refuses while .bee/HANDOFF.json exists, zero mutations
PASS  bee.mjs state start-feature refuses while a registered worker remains, zero mutations
PASS  bee.mjs state start-feature refuses while an active reservation remains, zero mutations; an expired one does not block
PASS  bee.mjs state start-feature refuses while ANY cell anywhere is claimed, zero mutations
PASS  bee.mjs state start-feature refuses while the PRIOR feature has a nonterminal (open/blocked) cell, and succeeds once each is dropped via the existing drop verb (P1 repair: no auto-clear cleanup)
PASS  bee.mjs state start-feature: a CAPPED prior-feature cell is terminal and never blocks (only open/claimed/blocked do)
PASS  bee.mjs state start-feature defaults --phase to "exploring" and --mode to null when omitted
PASS  bee.mjs state start-feature rejects --dry-run (a mutating verb, same generic guard as every non-prune verb)
PASS  bee.mjs status --json summarizes `lanes` by default (active lane in full + counts/ids for the rest); --lanes-full restores the full per-lane array; zero lanes on disk renders the empty shape either way; every pre-existing status field keeps its exact shape
PASS  readHandoff: no file -> null; missing/unknown kind normalizes to "pause" (fail-safe); an explicit planned-next kind is preserved
PASS  writeHandoff: --kind pause keeps today's free-form fields, adds kind + written_at, no preconditions
PASS  writeHandoff: refuses a missing/invalid --kind, zero mutation
PASS  writeHandoff: planned-next succeeds only when the previous cell is capped with verify_passed true AND the next cell's claim is owned by writer_session; stores writer_session/previous_cell/next_cell (must-have truth)
PASS  writeHandoff: planned-next refuses (typed, zero mutation) when the previous cell is not capped, or capped without verify_passed true (must-have truth)
PASS  writeHandoff: planned-next refuses (typed, zero mutation) when the next cell has no claim, or a claim owned by a DIFFERENT session (must-have truth)
PASS  adoptHandoff: transfers the carried claim to the adopting session then clears the handoff (success path)
PASS  adoptHandoff: refuses (typed, never throws) with no handoff present
PASS  adoptHandoff: a pause handoff is NEVER adopted — typed refusal, handoff left intact (D1 auto-resume boundary)
PASS  adoptHandoff: a failed claim adopt (e.g. the claim vanished underneath the handoff) is a typed refusal that leaves the handoff intact, never a throw
PASS  adoptHandoff: idempotent recovery — a crash between claim-adopt and handoff-clear self-heals on the next call (benign self-adopt then clear), never orphaning the claim
PASS  buildSessionPreamble: handoffOutcome omitted (null) renders a pause handoff identically whether or not a sessionId is bound — no start-now, no reason line ever fabricated
PASS  buildSessionPreamble: a planned-next handoff with no handoffOutcome (e.g. no session_id at all) renders the plain wait block — no start-now, no reason line — this is the fsh-10 no-session_id byte-parity contract
PASS  buildSessionPreamble: handoffOutcome.ok===true replaces the wait block with a start-now block naming the adopted cell, its lane, and its verify command (must-have truth)
PASS  buildSessionPreamble: handoffOutcome.ok===false renders the wait block plus one reason line — never a fabricated start-now (must-have truth)
PASS  race: two-session handoff — session A caps+claims+hands off, session B adopts across the simulated /clear boundary, and a concurrent third-session steal loses with typed CLAIMED (epic-map E4, riding fsh-2's race harness pattern)
PASS  si-1 (D1): state set --feature <different> is REFUSED while the CURRENT feature carries unpaid scribing debt — a swap has the same silent-abandonment effect as a close
PASS  si-1: --waive-scribing-debt permits a feature swap over unpaid debt and logs the waiver, same as a close
PASS  si-1: state set --feature <same value> is never treated as a swap — no debt check fires even with unpaid debt standing
PASS  si-1 (D2): a LANE close (state set --lane X --phase compounding-complete) checks the LANE feature's own debt — an idle/clean default record does not let a debt-carrying lane through
PASS  si-1: --waive-scribing-debt permits a lane close over unpaid lane debt and logs the waiver
PASS  si-1: a lane close with a scribing run stamped AFTER the lane cells were capped (the lane's own last_scribing_run) passes cleanly with no waiver
PASS  si-1: state scribing-run appends a parseable line to .bee/logs/scribing-runs.jsonl on every call
PASS  si-1: state scribing-run can stamp a NON-active feature — writes the ledger line but leaves the default record feature/phase untouched
PASS  tst-1: state scribing-run for a NON-active feature succeeds from phase "compounding-complete" — the phase gate never applies to a repair-path call that leaves the default record untouched
PASS  tst-1: state scribing-run for the ACTIVE feature still refuses from phase "compounding-complete" — the phase gate is unchanged for the run that actually advances the default record
PASS  si-1 (D5): status --json scribing_debt gains an ADDITIVE orphaned block {count, features} from the global sweep, independent of the active feature
PASS  sqs-b3: state scribing-run --show returns the most-recent stamp overall, across every feature in the ledger
PASS  sqs-b3: state scribing-run --show --feature <slug> returns that feature's own last stamp, not a different feature's later one
PASS  sqs-b3: state scribing-run --show is read-only — it does not append to the ledger and does not advance phase
PASS  sqs-b3: state scribing-run --show works with NO --areas/--next-action (a bare read never needs write-only flags)
PASS  sqs-b3: bee.mjs --help --json advertises scribing-run --show
PASS  si-3: this suite never mutates the LIVE repo .bee/state.json — its `feature` captured at suite start is unchanged at suite end (every fixture here uses its own scratch root; the isolation leak si-1 left behind, restored by hand, must never recur silently)
